_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: User research involves methods to identify user needs and understand how users interact with a product or service.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in measuring adoption and identifying adoption barriers, including usage analytics review, stakeholder interviews, and friction point diagnosis.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-005 (LS, cosine: 0.440), AB-T-001 (AB, cosine: 0.419).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.4/10 (raw 0.640). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DDaT-only terms [identify, interact, involves, methods, needs, product] vs WIDAI-only terms [adoption, analytics, barriers, diagnosis, friction, identifying] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.871 (P=0.873, R=0.869). Balanced P/R — the 18-word DDaT element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "User research involves methods to identify user needs and understand how users i" and "Skill in measuring adoption and identifying adoption barriers, including usage a". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.430 < STS 0.640 (gap -0.210). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [identify, interact, involves] and [adoption, analytics, barriers] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.1%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DDaT terms: [identify, interact, involves, methods, needs, product]. WIDAI terms: [adoption, analytics, barriers, diagnosis, friction, identifying].
